title: Analysis State Transition
description: v1 audit record for one analysis-run state transition (PRD section 32.1: transitions must be explicit, validated, persisted, and auditable; docs/PRODUCT_FLOW_AND_AI_BOUNDARIES.md canonical contract 'Analysis state transition'). The LEGALITY of a transition (allowed from->to pairs, preconditions) is enforced by the deterministic backend state machine, not by this schema; this schema fixes the audit-record shape. correlation_id is required for structured-log traceability (PRD section 25). TASK M2-T017 (DF-5, whole-system trust replan Area G): this audit record is now CLOSED with additionalProperties:false so it can no longer silently accept an undocumented or mistyped key - an unknown field (typo of the optional 'reason', or a leaked diagnostic/internal field) is rejected instead of persisted into an audit trail. No runtime writes this record yet (the deterministic state machine is a later task), so no conformant producer output is affected; the allowlist serializer in services/api/app/contracts/serializers.py is the frozen (un-wired) writer interface a future state machine will use.

run_id.description: Identifier of the analysis run. Every run has exactly one current state (PRD section 32.1).

from_state.description: Previous state; null only for the initial transition that creates the run in its first state.

actor.description: PLATFORM-DEFINED enum: 'system' = the deterministic backend state machine; 'user' = an authenticated user action that the backend validated. There is deliberately no 'ai' actor: the AI may not choose or skip workflow states (PRD section 32.1).

correlation_id.description: Correlation id linking this transition to structured logs and jobs (PRD section 25).

reason.description: Optional human-readable reason or triggering event (e.g. 'Geoclient /v2/search exact match').
